description:
You are a technical writer who keeps all project documentation under
  "/home/user/docs_local/".  
  For review-purposes you also maintain a *mirror copy* on an internal file
  server that, for the purpose of this exercise, is represented by the local
  directory "/home/user/remote_server/docs_remote/".

  Your job is to perform a **one-way synchronization** (local → “remote”) and to
  create two artefacts that the release-engineers can later audit:

  1. A detailed rsync transfer log (one line per file).  
  2. A very small summary file that is trivial for humans to read.

  Read the specification below **carefully**; the automated grader will verify
  every character of the resulting files.

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  Initial state of the file-tree (already present before you start):

    /home/user/docs_local/
      ├── index.md                          (UTF-8 text,  14 bytes)
      ├── .gitignore                        (UTF-8 text,   9 bytes)
      ├── README.md  ->  index.md           (symbolic link)
      ├── images/
      │     └── diagram.png                 (binary file,  0 bytes placeholder)
      └── intro/
            └── overview.md                 (UTF-8 text,  18 bytes)

    /home/user/remote_server/               (exists, but **docs_remote/**
                                              is empty)

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  What you must accomplish:

  STEP 1 – Create the destination directory  
          Make sure "/home/user/remote_server/docs_remote/" exists before you
          attempt the transfer.

  STEP 2 – Synchronize  
          Copy *everything* from "/home/user/docs_local/" to
          "/home/user/remote_server/docs_remote/".  
          Requirements:
            • Use rsync in archive mode so that permissions, time-stamps,
              hidden files (e.g. ".gitignore") and symbolic links are preserved.  
            • The command must produce an itemized change list
              (rsync option “--itemize-changes”) that you will redirect to a log
              file described in STEP 3.

  STEP 3 – Detailed log  
          Immediately after rsync finishes, write the transfer log to a file
          under "/home/user/sync_logs/".  
          File-name format (strictly):  
            sync_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log  
          where the timestamp is produced by the **same** invocation of
          `date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S` that you run while creating the file.  
          The log must contain EXACTLY the raw “--itemize-changes” lines that
          rsync prints to stdout, **unmodified**, one line per file.  
          Example of one line (actual letters may differ):  
              ">f++++++++ index.md"

  STEP 4 – Human-readable summary  
          Create the file "/home/user/sync_logs/latest_sync_summary.txt" that
          contains precisely two lines (no extra spaces, no blank line at end):

              Total files transferred: <N>
              Total bytes sent: <B>

          • <N>  is the number of files whose itemized-change line begins with
            the character “>”.  
          • <B>  is the “sent” byte-count reported by rsync on its final
            statistics line (just the integer, no “bytes” word).

  Constraints & verification rules (what the grader will check):

    • All files listed above must exist under
      "/home/user/remote_server/docs_remote/" after the sync, with identical
      contents and the symlink intact.  
    • The detailed log file must exist, its name must match the timestamp
      pattern, and it must contain at least five non-blank lines corresponding
      to the five paths copied on first run.  
    • "latest_sync_summary.txt" must exist and follow the
      two-line, no-trailing-space format *exactly*.  
    • The count <N> in the summary **must** equal the number of log lines that
      start with “>”.  
    • The byte-count <B> must be the integer reported by rsync (the grader
      will tolerate an exact textual match only).

  Tip: create "/home/user/sync_logs/" if it does not exist, and remember that
  you do **not** have root privileges, so stay inside "/home/user/".

  That’s it—perform the steps in a Linux terminal. The grader will evaluate the
  presence and correctness of the synchronized files and both log files.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data for automated grading.
    This information is NOT visible to the agent.
  -->
  <initial>
    <dir path="/home/user/docs_local/">
      <file name="index.md"      size="14">Hello, world.\n</file>
      <file name=".gitignore"    size="9">*.tmp\n</file>
      <file name="README.md"     type="symlink" target="index.md"/>
      <dir  name="images">
        <file name="diagram.png" size="0" binary="true"/>
      </dir>
      <dir  name="intro">
        <file name="overview.md" size="18">Project overview.\n</file>
      </dir>
    </dir>
    <dir path="/home/user/remote_server/">
      <!-- docs_remote intentionally absent / empty -->
    </dir>
  </initial>

  <expectations>
    <!-- After successful completion -->
    <dir path="/home/user/remote_server/docs_remote/">
      <file name="index.md"      sameas="/home/user/docs_local/index.md"/>
      <file name=".gitignore"    sameas="/home/user/docs_local/.gitignore"/>
      <file name="README.md"     type="symlink" target="index.md"/>
      <dir  name="images">
        <file name="diagram.png" sameas="/home/user/docs_local/images/diagram.png"/>
      </dir>
      <dir  name="intro">
        <file name="overview.md" sameas="/home/user/docs_local/intro/overview.md"/>
      </dir>
    </dir>

    <logs>
      <dir path="/home/user/sync_logs/">
        <file pattern="sync_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}\.log" lines=">=5"
              mustContain=">f|c|h"/>
        <file path="/home/user/sync_logs/latest_sync_summary.txt">
          <line1 pattern="Total files transferred: [0-9]+"/>
          <line2 pattern="Total bytes sent: [0-9]+"/>
          <exactlyTwoLines/>
        </file>
      </dir>
    </logs>
  </expectations>
